chapter_title.en: Prayer of the Sick, the Unconcious, the Weak and the Infirm, the Elderly and Others

text: 1050 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ مَيْسَرَةَ: أَنَّهُ سَأَلَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَ يُصَلِّي اَلرَّجُلُ وَ هُوَ جَالِسٌ مُتَرَبِّعٌ وَ مَبْسُوطُ اَلرِّجْلَيْنِ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِذَلِكَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1050 - Muawiya ibn Maysara narrated from  Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as): "Can a person pray while sitting in the position of tawarruk (cross-legged) or with his legs spread out?"
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in that."
